article_title: Farouk Kibet Gifts KSh 10k to Woman Who Named Baby after Him During Campaigns in Malava - Tuko.co.ke
content: Global site navigation Local editions Join channel on WhatsApp now! A woman who named her newborn baby Farouk Kibet screamed at the top of her lungs, danced, and shouted after the baby's namesake, President William Ruto's aide, met him. Farouk has been on the campaign trail in Malava Constituency to seek votes for the United Democratic Alliance UDA candidate, David Ndakwa, whose foremost challenger is Seth Panyako of DAP-K. Read also The seat fell vacant when its Member of Parliament, Malulu Injendi, at the Aga Khan Hospital in Nairobi, and several aspirants Farouk has been on the front line to drum up support for the candidate and has basically been camping in the area, endearing himself to the locals. As Farouk was moving around the village, he met the woman along the road, and the woman started screaming while moving close enough to touch him, not believing that she had met the famous man in flesh and blood. A bemused Farouk moved forward, held the baby in his arms for a few minutes, and then ordered a man standing beside him to give the woman KSh 10,000. She couldn't believe it and even became wilder, ululating at the top of her lungs and dancing while touching Farouk in disbelief. Khalwale also called out Farouk for meddling in the affairs of Malava, which is in and not Rift Valley, where he comes from. He told the president's aide not to let the constituents exercise their democratic right independently. Source TUKO.co.ke

article_title: Embu Woman Working as Househelp in Saudi Arabia Dies While Receiving Treatment - Tuko.co.ke
content: Global site navigation Local editions A young woman from who travelled abroad alive and in good health has unfortunately met her fate. Annette Kagendo left for greener pastures in Saudi Arabia, hoping to build a better future for herself and her family. She was stationed in Riyadh, where she worked at the Saudi Manpower Solutions Company SMASCO. Kagendo fell sick and was rushed to a medical facility to receive professional medical attention and care. Unfortunately, her health did not improve, and she sadly passed away in the hospital where she was receiving treatment. Read also Her identification card was shared as the hospital sought to find her family and contact them to inform them of their kins death. The hospital also shared the details with the Kenyan Embassy in their quest to reach the deceased's family. Another video showed the moment Kagendo's body was being repatriated from Saudi Arabia. The young lady's body was placed in a box as a man jotted down important information on it. Another person, seen in a blue apron, locked the padlock, ensuring the wooden box was secure for the journey. They later loaded the box into the trunk of a van as they prepared to transport it to the deceased's family for burial. Several social media users reacted to the heartbreaking news in the comment section. article_title: Nakuru Man Shattered as High School Student He Was to Marry after 2025 KCSE Dies - Tuko.co.ke
content: Global site navigation Local editions The dream of a young man from Rongai, , of starting a family has been shattered. Brian Rotich could not hold back his emotions after being told about the untimely death of his lover. He was set to marry Dorcas Cherono, but their wedding plans have now turned into burial preparations following her demise. Rotich, who was on his way to visit his girlfriends parents, recounted receiving the devastating news. Read also Rotich was to marry the 20-year-old Cherono on Thursday, December 18, 2025, at her village home in Kipkelion, . She was sitting for her 2025 Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education KCSE examinations and planned to get married afterwards. She asked them to stand so they could sing together, which became her second-last act of service to her Maker. Read also Cherono took a drum and began beating it as she sang with the young people, not knowing her heart would soon stop beating. Cherono asked them if they were prepared three times she repeated the question. Since she was in the praise and worship department, she said she needed praise and worship members. The 24-year-old said Cherono, who studied at Kipkelion Girls Secondary School, was quickly taken outside and given first aid before being rushed to hospital. At the medical facility, she was pronounced dead, leaving her family, friends, and church members in shock. Read also In another story, a newlywed Nairobi bride for her husband, who died 40 days after their wedding. Naombi Wambui was heartbroken after her boda boda rider husband, Frankline Gatukui, was attacked and killed by unknown assailants. The news of his demise brought a wave of grief to his friends, family, and community. Source TUKO.co.ke

article_title: Chinese Manager Caught Assaulting Worker At Eldoret Factory
content: A video circulating on social media shows a Chinese branch manager striking a Kenyan employee repeatedly with an iron rod at the TCM Mabati factory in Eldoret. The footage has provoked national outrage, prompted a police probe and raised fresh questions about worker protection at foreign-run sites. The footage shared widely on X, Facebook and WhatsApp appears to show a heated workplace confrontation inside the factory compound. In the clip the manager repeatedly hits the employee while the victim attempts to shield himself. The exact cause of the dispute is not yet public, but the video alone sparked immediate calls for law enforcement action. Local police confirmed they received reports about the incident and say they have opened investigations. Authorities are reviewing the footage, interviewing witnesses, and will determine whether to file charges. The suspect may face assault and workplace violence-related offences under Kenyan law. For general information on reporting workplace crime see the . In a surprising turn, the employee involved later told local reporters he had forgiven the manager and wished to keep his job. That statement, widely shared online, has divided public opinion some praise his forgiving stance while others say forgiveness should not replace accountability or proper legal redress. Kenyans responding online denounced the attack and demanded firm action. Comments ranged from calls for immediate deportation of the manager to demands for tighter workplace oversight. Many users expressed frustration about repeated reports of labour abuses in factories with foreign management, particularly in projects linked to overseas investors. If this was in Europe he would be in jail even after forgiveness, wrote one user. Another comment read This highlights weak enforcement of worker protections action must follow. Kenyan labour law protects employees from physical assault and unsafe workplaces. Employers are obliged to provide a safe working environment an

article_title: Nyaramba: Woman Allegedly Attacked by Kibuye Market Chairlady Reportedly Loses 2 Unborn Babies - Tuko.co.ke
content: Global site navigation Local editions The Kibuye Market assault case involving chairlady Judith Matengo, popularly known as Nyaramba, has taken a tragic new development after one of the victims reportedly miscarried. Mercy Okoth and Rozette Adhiambo were attacked by Nyaramba in the open market under circumstances that are yet to be fully established. A viral video showing the chairlady with a rod sparked outrage among traders and the wider public. Read also The incident, which occurred on November 17, 2025, left the victims with injuries and raised concerns about violence and intimidation within the busy market. Nyaramba was arrested shortly after the video circulated and arraigned at the Winam Law Courts, where she was charged with assault causing actual bodily harm, public disorder, and unlawful violence. The court ordered that she be detained at Kodiaga GK Womens Prison until November 24, when her bond application will be heard. Meanwhile, Mercy developed complications following the brutal assault. Local station Mayienga FM reported that she was three months pregnant at the time of the attack and has since lost her two unborn babies. The development has intensified calls for justice, with Kenyans demanding accountability. The miscarriage has further fueled public anger. Bigwig de Konaka Read also Allan Sparta Caroline Asienwa Herman Okumu Onyango Pauline Ongera Asindi Mary Meanwhile, Nyaramba has been active in for years, supporting different leaders across various eras. Reports indicate that she is now for the Kaloleni-Shauri Moyo ward in Kisumu Central. Read also Her past is marked by a violent market leadership dispute that left two people dead, others injured, and motorbikes destroyed. Initially elected as market chairlady for a three-year term, she sought court intervention as her tenure neared expiry in April 2024 and was granted an additional year. Source TUKO.co.ke

article_title: Family, Friends Mourn 3 High School Students Killed in Tragic Road Accident - Tuko.co.ke
content: Global site navigation Local editions Three families are heartbroken following the sudden and tragic loss of their young daughters. Mashishing Secondary School, in a statement shared on their social media channels, announced the deaths of the three students, leaving the entire community in mourning. The principal initially informed the parents via the schools WhatsApp group, announcing that the three girls, in Grades Nine, 10 and 12, had passed away in a road accident. He also stated that the school would honour the deceased students with a memorial at the Mashishing Community Hall in Lydenburg, , on November 20. Read also He also extended heartfelt to the families and friends of the victims on behalf of the school, offering prayers for strength, comfort, and healing during this profoundly painful time. Authorities have yet to announce the cause of the accident or provide further details on how the students died. The news of the tragedy left many in deep sorrow and sparked widespread reactions online, with many netizens expressing their condolences. Many tributes were shared online, with some reflecting on the deep emotional impact of losing loved ones and the difficulty of continuing life in the aftermath of such a loss. article_title: Magistrate Refuses to Convict Student Arraigned for Stealing Whiskey: "Maybe He Wanted to Unwind" - Tuko.co.ke
content: Global site navigation Local editions A court has drawn public attention after Chief Magistrate Stella Atambo declined to convict a young man accused of attempting to steal a bottle of whiskey. The 23-year-old student was alleged to have attempted to steal KSh1,800 from a Naivas Supermarket. Read also Chief Magistrate Atambo's decision followed a brief exchange filled with questions, advice, and a push for a practical solution instead of punishment. Once he entered the dock, the atmosphere shifted as Magistrate Atambo chose to speak to him directly rather than move straight into the charge. When she inquired about the value of the whiskey, the prosecution responded with KSh1,000 before she corrected and noted KSh1,800 appeared on the charge sheet. She reminded the young lad that a small mistake often grows into a long-term burden once recorded as a conviction. After asking and confirming after confirming his age, Atambo told him he still had a long future ahead and should avoid errors that remain on official files. The student told the court he preferred to settle the amount instead of facing jail time, but he lacked money since he was jobless. Read also When she asked whether anyone had come to support him with cash bail, he said a cousin was headed to court. She then shifted the discussion, explaining that she did wish to convict him over one bottle of John Barr whiskey. Turning to lawyers present in court, Atambo questioned what they could do for him since he was a young man who required direction. The advocates said they agreed with the magistrate, stating that the amount needed to be settled with the supermarket. Magistrate Atambo pressed them further and reminded them of their frequent claims about mentoring young people. Read also The magistrate seemed to justify the student's actions, adding that he likely wanted a brief moment to unwind and ended up making a poor choice. content: Global site navigation Local editions Disturbing revelations have surfaced about Judith Matengo, popularly known as Nyaramba, the Kibuye Market chairlady who was arrested after being with a rod. Although the motive behind the attack remained unclear, Nyaramba has been described as a politically protected figure with a history of controversy. According to an investigative piece by Ramogi TV, Nyaramba is alleged to walk around the market armed with crude weapons to intimidate traders. Read also Several traders accused the tomato trader of repeated harassment, claiming that attempts to confront her were thwarted by political shielding. Her past includes her alleged involvement in a violent dispute over market leadership and a clash over the market's toilet, which left two people dead, several injured, and motorbikes burnt. During that period, she and another man were battling for the chairmanship of Kibuye market. Read also Nyaramba was held in police custody for seven days after denying the assault charge at Winam Law Courts. However, influencer Hezborne Migott alleged that this was not her first time in court, recalling that her previous arraignment sparked violence in Kibuye, leading to fatalities. He claimed she was once detained at Kodiaga Prison but later bailed out by a political leader she returned to the market with what he described as 'presidential-like' influence. Her three-year tenure as market chairlady was set to end in April 2024, but she sought court intervention and was granted an additional year, halting the succession process. article_title: Video: Mike Sonko Rescues Another Woman Punched by Her Boxer Boyfriend, Pays Her Loan - Tuko.co.ke
content: Global site navigation Local editions Join channel on WhatsApp now! Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko has sustained the fight against GBV in the country. Barely two days after his own daughter, Salma Sonko, was , the former county boss has stepped up to lead a campaign against domestic violence. On Tuesday, November 18, Sonko received a distress call from a Mombasa-based woman asking for help after her sister was assaulted by her boyfriend, a renowned boxer in the Coastal city. Sonko asked to speak to the victim, who identified herself as Zuhura. Read also She claimed her boyfriend stormed into their rented house at around 10 pm and started assaulting her over KSh 16k he lent her in 2022. The boyfriend did not take any of her offers he proceeded to assault her and eject her from the house, where she said they shared rent expenses. She rushed to the Bamburi Police Station, where she reported the matter. According to a police officer who spoke with Sonko on the phone, Zuhura was forced out of the house unprepared and arrived at the station partially dressed. The distraught woman said her lover had also assaulted her in February 2023, but they reconciled even after reporting the incident to the police. Read also According to Zuhura's sister, the boxer doesn't have a formal job and has been depending on the victim for survival. She claimed the fighter, who relocated to Mombasa from an Arab nation, has a habit of assaulting her sister whenever he gets drunk. Sonko advised the woman to seek treatment and fill out a P3 form to pave the way for police arrest and investigation. Read also The former governor also pleaded with the police to accompany the distressed lady to her house and relocate her to a safe place, a request they accepted. The boyfriend who attempted to escape with some household items was traced and nabbed by the officers. Source TUKO.co.ke
